#define XV_MULTI_SCALER_SRC_IMG_BUF_0 (   x)    XV_multi_scaler_Set_HwReg_srcImgBuf0_##x##_V

Macro to generate function name for setting source image buffer 0 of channel x.

#define XV_MULTI_SCALER_SRC_IMG_BUF_1 (   x)    XV_multi_scaler_Set_HwReg_srcImgBuf1_##x##_V

Macro to generate function name for setting source image buffer 1 of channel x.

#define XV_MULTI_SCALER_STRIDE_IN (   x)    XV_multi_scaler_Set_HwReg_InStride_##x

Macro to generate function name for setting input stride of channel x.

#define XV_MULTI_SCALER_STRIDE_OUT (   x)    XV_multi_scaler_Set_HwReg_OutStride_##x

Macro to generate function name for setting output stride of channel x.

#define XV_MULTI_SCALER_WIDTH_IN (   x)    XV_multi_scaler_Set_HwReg_WidthIn_##x

Macro to generate function name for setting input width of channel x.

#define XV_MULTI_SCALER_WIDTH_OUT (   x)    XV_multi_scaler_Set_HwReg_WidthOut_##x

Macro to generate function name for setting output width of channel x.

  RegOffset,
  Data 
)    Xil_Out32((BaseAddress) + (RegOffset), (u32)(Data))

Typedef Documentation

typedef void(* XVMultiScaler_Callback)(void *CallbackRef)

Multi Scaler callback function type.

Function Documentation

int XV_multi_scaler_CfgInitialize ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
XV_multi_scaler_Config *  ConfigPtr 
)

Initialize the Multi Scaler core instance.

This function initializes the Multi Scaler driver/device instance with the configuration parameters from the provided config structure. It copies configuration parameters and sets the instance to ready state.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance to be worked on
ConfigPtris a pointer to the configuration structure containing hardware build-time parameters
Returns
XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful
Note
The calling application must memset the instance structure to zero before passing it to this function

References XV_multi_scaler::Config, XV_multi_scaler_Config::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler_Config::IntrId, XV_multi_scaler_Config::IntrParent, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_multi_scaler_Config::MaxCols, XV_multi_scaler::MaxCols, XV_multi_scaler_Config::MaxDataWidth, XV_multi_scaler::MaxDataWidth, XV_multi_scaler_Config::MaxOuts, XV_multi_scaler::MaxOuts, XV_multi_scaler_Config::MaxRows, XV_multi_scaler::MaxRows, XV_multi_scaler_Config::NumTaps, XV_multi_scaler::NumTaps, XV_multi_scaler_Config::PhaseShift, XV_multi_scaler::PhaseShift, XV_multi_scaler_Config::SamplesPerClock, XV_multi_scaler::SamplesPerClock, XV_multi_scaler_Config::ScaleMode, and XV_multi_scaler::ScaleMode.

Referenced by XV_multi_scaler_Initialize().

void XV_multi_scaler_DisableAutoRestart ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Disable auto-restart mode for Multi Scaler.

This function disables the auto-restart feature, requiring manual restart after each frame processing operation completes.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_AP_CTRL, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by XV_MultiScalerStop().

void XV_multi_scaler_EnableAutoRestart ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Enable auto-restart mode for Multi Scaler.

This function enables the auto-restart feature, allowing the Multi Scaler to automatically restart processing after completing the current frame.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_AP_AUTO_RESTART_BIT_MASK, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_AP_CTRL, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by XV_MultiScalerStart().

u64 XV_multi_scaler_Get_HwReg_dstImgBuf0_0_V ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Get destination image buffer 0 address for channel 0.

This function reads the 64-bit address of destination image buffer 0 for channel 0 from the hardware registers.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
The 64-bit buffer address
Note
None

int XV_multi_scaler_Initialize ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
UINTPTR  BaseAddress 
)

Initialize the Multi Scaler driver instance using base address.

This function locates the device configuration based on the base address and calls the configuration initialization function. The instance must be initialized before using any other driver functions. The base address is typically obtained from the device tree.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance to be initialized.
BaseAddressis the physical base address of the device.
Returns
XST_SUCCESS if initialization was successful, XST_DEVICE_NOT_FOUND if the configuration for the base address was not found.
Note
This function is used in SDT (Software Development Toolkit) builds.

References XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_multi_scaler_CfgInitialize(), and XV_multi_scaler_LookupConfig().

Referenced by main().

void XV_multi_scaler_InterruptClear ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
u32  Mask 
)

Clear pending interrupts.

This function clears the interrupt status bits specified by the mask parameter. This should be called in the interrupt handler to acknowledge processed interrupts.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Maskis the bitmask of interrupt sources to clear
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_ISR, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by XV_MultiScalerIntrHandler().

void XV_multi_scaler_InterruptDisable ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
u32  Mask 
)

Disable specific interrupt sources.

This function disables interrupt sources specified by the mask parameter. Multiple sources can be disabled by ORing the appropriate bits.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Maskis the bitmask of interrupt sources to disable
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_IER, XV_multi_scaler_ReadReg, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by XVMultiScalerCallback().

void XV_multi_scaler_InterruptEnable ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
u32  Mask 
)

Enable specific interrupt sources.

This function enables interrupt sources specified by the mask parameter. Multiple sources can be enabled by ORing the appropriate bits.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Maskis the bitmask of interrupt sources to enable
Returns
None

void XV_multi_scaler_InterruptGlobalDisable ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Disable global interrupts for Multi Scaler.

This function disables the global interrupt enable bit, preventing interrupts from the Multi Scaler from reaching the system.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_GIE, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by XVMultiScalerCallback().

void XV_multi_scaler_InterruptGlobalEnable ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Enable global interrupts for Multi Scaler.

This function enables the global interrupt enable bit, allowing interrupts from the Multi Scaler to be propagated to the system.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_GIE, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by main().

u32 XV_multi_scaler_IsDone ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Check if Multi Scaler processing is done.

This function checks the AP_DONE bit in the control register to determine if the Multi Scaler has completed the current frame processing.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
Non-zero if the core has finished processing the current frame, zero otherwise

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_AP_DONE_BIT_MASK, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_AP_CTRL, and XV_multi_scaler_ReadReg.

u32 XV_multi_scaler_IsIdle ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Check if Multi Scaler is idle.

This function checks the AP_IDLE bit in the control register to determine if the Multi Scaler hardware is currently idle (not processing).

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
Non-zero if the core is idle, zero if it is actively processing

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_AP_IDLE_BIT_MASK, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_AP_CTRL, and XV_multi_scaler_ReadReg.

u32 XV_multi_scaler_IsReady ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Check if Multi Scaler is ready for next operation.

This function checks if the Multi Scaler is ready to accept new input by verifying that the AP_START bit is not set in the control register.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
Non-zero if the core is ready for next input, zero otherwise

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_AP_START_BIT_MASK, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_AP_CTRL, and XV_multi_scaler_ReadReg.

XV_multi_scaler_Config* XV_multi_scaler_LookupConfig ( UINTPTR  BaseAddress)

Lookup the device configuration by base address.

This function searches the configuration table for a device matching the specified base address. The configuration table is populated at compile time based on the device tree configuration. If BaseAddress is 0, the first entry in the table is returned.

Parameters
BaseAddressis the physical base address of the device. If 0, returns the first configuration entry.
Returns
Pointer to the configuration structure if found, NULL otherwise.
Note
This function is used in SDT (Software Development Toolkit) builds.

References XV_multi_scaler_Config::Name, and XV_multi_scaler_ConfigTable.

Referenced by XV_multi_scaler_Initialize().

u32 XV_multi_scaler_Read_HwReg_mm_hfltCoeff_0_Bytes ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
int  offset,
char *  data,
int  length 
)

Read bytes from horizontal filter coefficient memory bank 0.

This function reads individual bytes from the horizontal filter coefficient memory bank 0 starting at the specified offset.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
offsetis the byte offset from the base address
datais a pointer to the buffer to store the read bytes
lengthis the number of bytes to read
Returns
Number of bytes read, or 0 if the read would exceed memory bounds
Note
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_HWREG_MM_HFLTCOEFF_0_BASE, and XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_HWREG_MM_HFLTCOEFF_0_HIGH.

int *  data,
int  length 
)

Read words from horizontal filter coefficient memory bank 2.

This function reads integer words from the horizontal filter coefficient memory bank 2 starting at the specified offset.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
offsetis the word offset from the base address
datais a pointer to the buffer to store the read data
lengthis the number of words to read
Returns
Number of words read, or 0 if the read would exceed memory bounds
Note
None

void XV_multi_scaler_Start ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr)

Start the Multi Scaler core.

This function starts the Multi Scaler hardware by setting the AP_START bit in the control register. Auto-restart mode is preserved if previously set.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
Returns
None

References XV_multi_scaler::Ctrl_BaseAddress, XV_multi_scaler::IsReady, XV_MULTI_SCALER_AP_AUTO_RESTART_BIT_MASK, XV_MULTI_SCALER_AP_START_BIT_MASK, XV_MULTI_SCALER_CTRL_ADDR_AP_CTRL, XV_multi_scaler_ReadReg, and XV_multi_scaler_WriteReg.

Referenced by XV_MultiScalerStart().

int  offset,
char *  data,
int  length 
)

Write bytes to horizontal filter coefficient memory bank 0.

offsetis the byte offset from the base address
datais a pointer to the byte array to write
lengthis the number of bytes to write

None

u32 XV_multi_scaler_Write_HwReg_mm_vfltCoeff_4_Bytes ( XV_multi_scaler *  InstancePtr,
int  offset,
char *  data,
int  length 
)

Write bytes to vertical filter coefficient memory bank 4.

This function writes individual bytes to the vertical filter coefficient memory bank 4 starting at the specified offset.

Parameters
InstancePtris a pointer to the Multi Scaler instance
offsetis the byte offset from the base address
datais a pointer to the byte array to write
lengthis the number of bytes to write
Returns
Number of bytes written, or 0 if the write would exceed memory bounds
Note
None
